results.title: Addressing Dangerous and Careless Driving
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, Traffic Police recorded an average of 190 dangerous driving and 18,000 careless driving offences annually. The Government employs a four-pronged approach involving enforcement, penalties, public education, and infrastructure improvements. Specific actions include activating speed enforcement in red-light cameras and deploying new Traffic Violation Enforcement Cameras at accident-prone areas. Effective 1 January 2026, the Ministry of Home Affairs raised fines and demerit points for speeding violations. Further measures to enhance road safety are under consideration and will be announced when ready.

results.title: Application and Success Rates of Special Approval for Additional Subsidies by Non-working Main Applicants since 2023
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, approximately 3% of Basic and Additional Subsidy recipients applied for Special Approval, with common reasons including caregiving, job seeking, training, pregnancy, and illness. The approval rate for these applications has remained high at around 90%. To better support lower-income families, full childcare subsidies are now extended to households earning $6,000 or below regardless of the applicant's working status. Additionally, families under the HDB Public Rental Scheme and MSF ComCare schemes are automatically eligible for maximum preschool subsidies without regard to working status.

results.title: Addressing Situation Where Foreign Spouses of Lower-income Elderly Singaporeans Cannot Work without LTVP
results.summary: ICA assesses Long-Term Visit Pass applications for foreign spouses of Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents based on marriage duration, children, financial sustainability, adverse records, and declared caregiving needs. The authority explicitly considers specific caregiving needs alongside these factors. Officials state that every application must avoid requiring the Singaporean taxpayer to pay more eventually. The response does not confirm whether ICA has modelled fiscal benefits of conditional LTVPs tied to employment versus long-term institutional care costs.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: The debate was adjourned at the request of Ms Indranee Rajah, with proceedings scheduled to resume the following day. No new policy measures, legislative amendments, or operational constraints were introduced during this session. The House did not deliberate on any specific bills or government proposals before concluding the sitting.

results.title: Assessing Secondhand Smoke Exposure in Open Common Areas and Additional Measures to Protect Residents
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment has not conducted assessments of secondhand smoke exposure in open-to-sky common areas where smoking is permitted. While acknowledging that residents on lower floors may experience exposure, the Ministry urges smokers to be considerate and refrain from prolonged smoking in areas affecting nearby residents. The Ministry stated it will consider public feedback as it continues to review smoking prohibition policies, with no specific buffer zones or distance requirements currently proposed.

results.title: Assessment of Effectiveness and Extension of Courier Hub Scheme to More Estates
results.summary: The Urban Redevelopment Authority and HDB launched the Courier Hub Scheme in September 2024 to improve last-mile delivery efficiency in residential estates. As of 16 February 2026, 30 applications have been received for the 89 multi-storey car parks currently offered, an increase from the initial 50. The scheme allows courier companies to use designated car park lots for parcel sorting and dispatch, enabling residents to receive parcels faster with fewer delivery vehicle trips. While the Ministry monitors the scheme's effectiveness, it will not make the scheme mandatory for all parcel sorting activities due to constraints on available car parks and the need to prioritize residents' parking needs and mitigate noise impacts on surrounding blocks.

results.title: Assessment of Employment Opportunities, Wage Growth and Shift Towards Technology-driven Jobs Given Declining Job Creation Per Dollar of Investment
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister explained that the 2025 decline in jobs per dollar of investment reflects a structural shift toward capital-intensive, technology-driven industries where fewer workers generate higher value-add. While hiring projections are conservative due to global uncertainty, the 15,700 jobs committed over the next five years are primarily for professional, managerial, and technician roles, with two-thirds expected to command a gross monthly salary above $5,000. The Government will anchor high-value investments in key sectors, strengthen the local talent pipeline through partnerships with institutes of higher learning, and support SMEs via the Partnerships for Capability Transformation scheme and Enterprise Innovation Scheme. Additionally, initiatives like the Career Conversion Programmes and enhanced SkillsFuture Workforce Development Grant support will be rolled out from 2026 to help redesign jobs and reskill workers for growth areas.

results.title: Audits on Contractors' Maintenance of Speed Enforcement Cameras and Penalties for Lapses
results.summary: Traffic Police conduct monthly audits on all speed enforcement cameras to verify accurate speed settings. Contractors facing lapses resulting in erroneous summons incur financial penalties or face contract termination per contractual agreements. Motorists may dispute speeding offences by submitting appeals via the Singapore Police Force's e-services. Upon review suggesting anomalies, Traffic Police verify camera settings and engage contractors to fix equipment if necessary.

results.title: Average Time for Completing Food Product Recalls
results.summary: Under the Singapore Food Agency's (SFA) recall framework, implicated businesses must remove affected products from all distribution points within 48 hours of receiving a direction and report completion to SFA. SFA subsequently verifies compliance by checking premises. Failure to comply constitutes an offence subject to prosecution. In the past three years, no enforcement action was taken against businesses issued recall directions as all complied with the 48-hour requirement. SFA will investigate and take relevant enforcement actions if affected products are found for sale after a recall.

results.title: Building Strong Pipeline of Cybersecurity Talent Across All Sectors
results.summary: The Government plays an enabling role in sustaining a cybersecurity talent pipeline, with Critical Information Infrastructure owners (CIIOs) remaining accountable for their systems' security. To support CIIOs, the Government organises nationwide exercises like Exercise Cyber Star and Critical Infrastructure Defence Exercise (CIDeX) to enhance skills across public and private sectors. For leadership development, the Cyber Security Agency (CSA) partners with Singapore Management University to deliver the Cybersecurity Strategic Leadership Programme for senior leaders. Early-stage talent cultivation includes the CSA's Cybersecurity Development Programme for graduates and the Digital and Intelligence Service's Sentinel Programme alongside CSA's SG Cyber Youth for students seeking hands-on experience in web programming, network forensics, and penetration testing. The Government will continue monitoring these efforts to ensure national capabilities evolve with emerging threats.

results.title: Capture of Data Based on Retiree, Disabled Persons and Single-parent Households, and on Housing Categories Despite Change in Government's Income Trends Report
results.summary: The Government updated the Key Household Income Trends report to include resident households with solely non-employed persons aged 65 and over, covering 49.3% of the lowest income decile. Disaggregated market income data for these retiree households is available on the Department of Statistics' website. Conversely, market income data for lone-parent households and households with members having difficulty performing basic activities is not published annually but is reported in the Census and General Household Survey conducted every five years. The Minister explained that expanding the report's scope ensures a complete representation of resident households and their income trends amidst Singapore's changing demographics.

results.title: Challenges Affecting Uptake of Graduate Industry Traineeship (GRIT) Scheme and Successful Job Placement Outcomes
results.summary: The Graduate Industry Traineeship (GRIT) scheme, launched in October 2025, provisioned 800 vacancies but saw applications fall by approximately 90% compared to its launch, with only about 400 of the 800 places filled to date. This uptake was influenced by graduates securing full-time employment or pursuing other opportunities before accepting offers, rather than scheme design constraints. The programme targets graduates needing extra support in an uncertain hiring climate, with vacancies sourced from growth sectors including small and medium enterprises (SMEs). A key operational feature allows host organisations to receive continued subsidies if they hire trainees who have completed at least three months of the traineeship, incentivizing conversion to permanent roles before the programme ends. As the first batch commenced in December 2025, no conversions have occurred yet.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for National Development
results.summary: The Senior Parliamentary Secretary confirmed that fire safety standards remain sufficient under temporary higher occupancy caps for larger private residential estates. These elevated occupancy rates apply exclusively to properties of at least 90 square metres, a size equivalent to a typical four-room HDB flat. The speaker issued a factual correction to a previous statement made on 25 February 2026, changing the minimum area requirement from 90 square kilometres to 90 square metres. No further details regarding implementation timelines or specific eligibility criteria were provided in this exchange.

results.title: Consular Assistance for Singaporeans Detained by US Border and Immigration Services in 2024 and 2025
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs provides consular assistance to Singaporeans detained by US Customs and Border Protection or Immigration and Customs Enforcement. In 2024 and 2025, eight cases of such detention were recorded where assistance was sought. The Minister confirmed that all eligible Singaporeans in these situations received support from the Ministry. No further details on the specific nature of the detentions or outcomes were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Continued Use of Facsimile for Communications with Government Agencies, and Pursuit of Digital and Safer Alternatives
results.summary: The Ministry of Digital Development and Information does not track fax usage prevalence between law firms and agencies like HDB, CPF Board, SLA, or banks. While law firms generally prefer email or online channels, the Ministry has no plans to phase out fax as a policy measure. Implementation of digital alternatives is left to individual organisations to decide based on their specific requirements. Consequently, there are no stated timelines for mandatory transitions or specific operational constraints imposed by the government regarding fax usage.

results.title: Correlation between Students' Use of AI and Cognitive Skills Decline
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) lacks specific Singapore data correlating student AI usage with cognitive skill decline but is currently conducting studies on AI's impact on learning. International research indicates that inappropriate AI use can cause over-reliance, negatively affecting basic recall and higher-order thinking. To prevent this, AI is introduced progressively from Primary 4 under teacher supervision, with technology used lightly in lower primary to prioritise hands-on inquiry. At the secondary level, students use AI independently via personal devices for well-designed tasks that personalise learning while guarding against cognitive outsourcing. Teachers receive MOE-developed AI tools with pedagogical guardrails and undergo professional learning to design lessons that foster fundamental knowledge and higher-order thinking. Proctored examinations disallowing AI use ensure students maintain strong fundamentals, while teacher interactions monitor progress to support learning gaps. MOE also develops students' AI literacy and guides ethical use to ensure AI benefits learning outside school.

results.title: Cost and Funding of Free Shuttle Bus Service in Gek Poh and Bus Utilisation Rates since 2025
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) provides a free daily shuttle bus between Gek Poh Shopping Centre and Hong Kah Point Neighbourhood Centre to maintain resident access during centre upgrades expected to finish in the third quarter of 2028. This service, costing approximately $14,000 monthly as part of the upgrading tender, is not a new practice but a temporary measure distinct from the Marine Parade service. Utilisation is high during weekday mornings and weekends at noon. The Minister noted that preliminary studies or learning points from Marine Parade were not considered due to the different context.

results.title: Criteria for Commencing Police Investigation on Report of Voluntarily Causing Hurt or Advising Victim to File Magistrate's Complaint
results.summary: The Police initiate Voluntarily Causing Hurt (VCH) investigations when harm to public interest/safety exists, victims are vulnerable, or aggravating factors like repeat offences are present; otherwise, victims are advised to file Magistrate's Complaints. Between 2023 and 2025, approximately 14,000 VCH cases were reported annually, with roughly 30% resulting in police investigations. The Police do not track the specific origin of investigations (police-initiated versus magistrate-directed). Criteria explicitly account for repeated assaults by the same perpetrator as an aggravating factor warranting investigation.

results.title: Cumulative Noise Cap Implementation for Concurrent Infrastructure Projects with North-South Corridor Project
results.summary: The National Environment Agency will coordinate with the Land Transport Authority to manage overlapping construction schedules for the North-South Corridor project if cumulative noise levels exceed regulatory limits. Enforcement actions will be taken against individual sites violating these limits. Noise barriers are mandatory for projects within 75 metres of residential premises, and localised noise impact assessments must be conducted before and during construction. Contractors must implement additional mitigation measures if required. The Minister did not confirm a cumulative noise cap or a review of the 70-metre distance rule for Nuovo and Castle Green condominiums.

results.title: Data on AI Singapore's AI Apprenticeship Programme's Graduate Placement in Last Three Years and Transparency on Hiring Practices
results.summary: The Minister directed Members to a written reply dated 24 February 2026 regarding the AI Apprenticeship Programme (AIAP) rather than providing immediate oral figures on graduate placement rates, internal hiring percentages, or staff alumni status. The written response details how the programme supports professional development and real-world AI deployment through hands-on project work with companies. Specific statistics on market absorption versus internal re-hiring were not stated in this transcript.

results.title: Data on Appeals Outcomes of HDB Sellers Affected by Ethnic Integration Policy
results.summary: In 2025, the Housing and Development Board received 657 appeals from flat owners facing selling difficulties due to the Ethnic Integration Policy (EIP). HDB acceded to 208 appeals, granting extended selling periods or waiving EIP limits in exceptional cases. As a last resort, the Board bought back five flats for eligible owners who exhausted other options. By 31 December 2025, HDB owned approximately 100 units acquired through EIP buyback assistance or compulsory acquisition. These units will be progressively offered for sale under the Sale of Balance Flats exercise and open booking of flats. The Minister clarified that these measures support racial harmony while assisting owners with genuine selling challenges when EIP quotas are reached.

results.title: Data on Disputes in Private Residential Estates from 2021 to 2025, and Improved Support for MCST Councils in Estate Management and Dispute Resolutions
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, applications to the Strata Titles Board remained stable at an average of 120 cases annually, while complaints to the Commissioner of Buildings declined from over 3,000 in 2021 to fewer than 900 in 2025. The Government is reviewing the Building (Strata Management) Act to enhance financial oversight, strengthen self-governance, and improve dispute resolution processes for Management Corporation Strata Title councils. Specific details regarding the review timeline and implementation plans will be shared when ready.

results.title: Data on E-Vaporiser Usage among New Arrested Drug Abusers
results.summary: The Central Narcotics Bureau does not track statistics on e-vaporiser usage among new drug abusers arrested. Consequently, the Ministry for Home Affairs cannot provide prevalence data for this specific demographic. The requested information is unavailable as the agency does not maintain records on this metric.

results.title: Data on Space Within REIT-owned Malls Occupied by Retail Brands, Broken Down by Brands' Country of Origin
results.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Industry does not collect or provide data on the percentage share of Gross Floor Area or unit counts occupied by retail brands in REIT-owned malls, broken down by brand country of origin. This absence of data stems from the fact that tenancy mix and tenant profiles are determined by commercial decisions of property owners and tenants reflecting market factors like consumer demand. Consequently, the Government cannot supply the requested comparison for end-2022 or the most recent figures, though it continues to monitor aggregated market trends through industry engagement.

results.title: Data on Total Number and Load Capacity of Data Centres in Singapore
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information confirmed that Singapore's total data centre capacity exceeds 1.4 gigawatts. The country currently hosts more than 70 data centres, comprising cloud, enterprise, and co-location facilities. Specific details on individual centre capacities were not provided in the response.

results.title: Dedicated Support for Singaporeans to Work Abroad to Help Develop Global Leadership Pipeline
results.summary: The Government supports overseas postings exceeding three years through programmes like the Overseas Markets Immersion Programme (OMIP) and International Postings Programme, which mandate a minimum six-month duration to ensure meaningful integration. Transitional support is provided via the Economic Development Board's Singapore Global Network for networking and the Overseas Transition Support programme for pre-departure cultural training. Companies must outline a career development plan covering the next 24 months, including post-posting pathways, to encourage retention of high-potential employees. While specific durations depend on company needs and individual preferences, these measures balance local talent retention with developing a global leadership pipeline.

results.title: Differences in PISA Performance across Socio-economic Groups
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that students from higher socio-economic status (SES) homes outperform peers from lower SES homes on average in PISA assessments, a trend consistent across all participating OECD systems. Statistically significant performance gaps between the bottom and top 25% of SES groups have remained largely stable in Singapore from 2012 to 2022. The Ministry monitors these differences, noting that while lower SES students perform strongly relative to OECD peers, the domestic gap between high and low SES groups has not narrowed significantly over the decade.

results.title: Differentiating between Wealth Held by Households in Public Versus in Private Housing, and Measures to Sustain or Improve Upward Mobility Rates
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance released Singapore's first wealth Gini coefficient, noting that the top 1% holds 14% of total household wealth while the top 5% holds 33%, with data derived from the Household Expenditure Survey combined with administrative records. The Minister clarified that the Gini coefficient measures net worth stock rather than liquidity, so it does not differentiate between public and private housing, owner-occupied versus investment properties, or local versus overseas assets. The next survey cycle is scheduled for 2028, and the Government will track trends to inform evidence-based policies rather than relying on absolute thresholds. While the Ministry remains open to shifting the tax burden toward wealth over time as society ages, it currently treats all property as a store of value without separating investment from owner-occupied holdings.

results.title: Divergence in Costs Incurred by Labour-intensive Domestic Sectors and Outward-facing Industries
results.summary: In 2025, unit labour costs rose in domestically-oriented sectors while declining in outward-oriented sectors due to weaker productivity growth in the former. To address cost pressures, the Government will provide a Corporate Income Tax Rebate and Cash Grant offering up to $30,000 in benefits for the Year of Assessment 2026. Additional support includes the Enterprise Development Grant for capability building and innovation, alongside the Progressive Wage Model in retail and food services to incentivise skill upgrades. The Government emphasises that businesses must transform and upgrade to maintain long-term competitiveness, noting that financial assistance alone is insufficient without structural changes.

results.title: Effect of Employment Pass Qualifying Salary and Other Workforce Policies on Skills Gaps and Wage Growth
results.summary: Foreign workforce policies, including Employment Pass qualifying salaries and the Complementarity Assessment Framework (COMPASS), aim to maintain Singapore's global competitiveness while supporting local wage growth and job creation. Real median income for full-time employed residents rose 4.1% in 2025, with resident unemployment stable at around 3% over the last decade. Between 2015 and 2025, resident PMET jobs grew by 357,000 compared to an 8,000 increase in EP and S Pass holders in growth sectors. The COMPASS Shortage Occupation List awards bonus points to address skills gaps, with three Infocomm Technology occupations removed in 2026 as local workers filled these roles. The Ministry of Manpower reviews the shortage occupation list annually, considering industry commitments to develop the local talent pipeline.

results.title: Effectiveness of Dadah Itu Haram Campaign in Changing Attitudes Towards Recreational Drug Use
results.summary: The Dadah Itu Haram (DIH) campaign, launched in April 2017, expanded its focus from awareness to encouraging mutual care and healthy lifestyles following a 2020 review. Supported by the Islamic Religious Council of Singapore and 70 mosques, the campaign engaged an estimated 4,000 participants annually via events like futsal and cycling. A 2025 National Drug Perception Survey indicates increasing conservative attitudes among youths, with 87.7% viewing drug abuse as harmful compared to 83.2% in 2023. The Ministry of Home Affairs and National Council Against Drug Abuse conduct regular surveys to track public perceptions, though specific longitudinal research on attitude changes among Malay/Muslim youth since 2017 was not commissioned or made available in the response.

results.title: Effectiveness of New Barrier-free Parking System in HDB Car Parks
results.summary: HDB is trialling a barrier-free parking system at eight car parks to improve the parking experience for residents and motorists. The system sends pre-enforcement alerts to motorists who park in unauthorised lots, informing them to shift their vehicles before a notice of parking offence is issued. This measure has resulted in a decrease of close to 60% in illegal parking cases. HDB will continue to refine the system and study other technologies to further enhance the parking experience at HDB car parks.

results.title: Expanding Day Activity Centre Capacity for Young Adults with Autism and Additional Support for Caregiving Families
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development is expanding Day Activity Centre capacity by 500 places, with tiered funding prioritizing clients with moderate to severe autism. Long-term care subsidies will be enhanced from July 2026 for families using adult disability services, including transport and DACs, alongside an Assistive Technology Fund enhancement effective January 2026. For individuals on the waitlist, SG Enable facilitates referrals to facilities with available capacity and supports engagement through Enabling Services Hubs and the Supported Transition and Engagement Programme. Caregivers can access subsidised respite services via the Take-A-Break programme to ease long-term caregiving burdens.

results.title: Expanding National Genetic Testing Programme Beyond Single-Gene Disorders
results.summary: The Ministry of Health adopts a disease-centric approach to genetic testing, prioritizing conditions with established gene-disease relationships and proven interventions. While the National Genetic Testing Programme currently covers single-gene disorders, the Ministry plans to expand it to common multifactorial conditions like Type II diabetes. This expansion will occur in due course as the Ministry explores additional diseases to facilitate early preventive interventions.

results.title: Impact of 2022 Commercial Strata Subdivision Changes on Central Area Property Markets for SMEs
results.summary: Since the 2022 restrictions on strata subdivision in Central Area prominent locations like Orchard Road, the market has remained stable with median prices rising 0.4% annually (2022–2025) versus a 0.8% annual decline pre-COVID. Transaction volumes averaged 325 units yearly post-2022, slightly higher than the 290 units recorded between 2016 and 2019. The rules apply only to specific developments under the Central Business District and Strategic Development Incentive Schemes, leaving other areas unaffected. SMEs continue accessing diverse commercial spaces across the Central Area at various price points, with no new measures currently considered to address affordability.

results.title: Impact of Baby Bonus and Parental Leave Enhancements on Fertility Rate in 2023 and 2025
results.summary: The Minister stated that assessing the impact of individual 2023 and 2025 Baby Bonus and parental leave enhancements on the fertility rate is difficult, as these Marriage and Parenthood measures are designed to work in combination. Surveys indicate these policies have been positively received, with parents noting that financial support and increased leave time encourage childbirth and aid their parenthood journey. The government regularly monitors policy efficacy and will continue reviewing the M&P package to better support families.

results.title: Implication for Design of Social Transfer Schemes with Change in Household Income Reporting to Include Non-employment Data
results.summary: The Government confirmed there are no immediate plans to shift major social transfer schemes like ComCare, Silver Support, Workfare, and the Assurance Package from relying on employment income for means-testing. While ComCare already employs a holistic assessment including non-employment income sources, the Minister stated that scheme parameters will continue to be reviewed to ensure effectiveness. No specific timeline or new eligibility rules for non-employment data reporting were announced.

results.title: Investigation into Firms under Transboundary Haze Pollution Act for Recent Haze Due To Peat Fires in Johor
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed that Singapore has not received any requests from the Johor State Government or Malaysian federal government for assistance regarding recent peat fires in Johor. Consequently, no firms inside or outside Singapore are currently being investigated under the Transboundary Haze Pollution Act 2014 in relation to these specific fires. The government stated it is in touch with Malaysian counterparts and will continue to monitor the situation. Details on the combined oral reply for the first question were previously provided on 12 February 2026.

results.title: Leveraging SAF's Contracted Food Services and Cookhouse Models for School Meal Provisions
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) engaged the Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) to learn from its food service experience while developing the Central Kitchen Meal Model (CKMM) for schools. MOE incorporated relevant elements of the SAF's "Design, Equip and Operate" model, requiring operators to design, equip, and operate canteen facilities. However, MOE's requirements differ from SAF's, specifically allowing independent meal selection and meeting distinct dietary needs for children. The CKMM is not intended for universal scaling but will be offered selectively to schools facing severe difficulties securing stall holders. MOE continues to explore options to refine the model and best serve students' needs.

results.title: License Suspensions due to Erroneously Issued Speeding Tickets and Recourse Available for Affected Vocational Drivers
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that no drivers have had their licenses suspended due to the 1,523 erroneously issued speeding tickets. Consequently, there are no affected taxi or private hire vehicle drivers requiring recourse or financial restitution for livelihood loss related to this specific issue. As no suspensions occurred, no operational constraints or eligibility conditions for compensation apply to vocational drivers in this context.

results.title: Maintaining Camp Security In Light of Recent Fake Bomb Threat Incident Involving Paya Lebar Air Base
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence directed Members to refer to the reply to Parliamentary Question No 7, asked by Mr Yip Hon Weng, for the Sitting on 24 February 2026, for details on maintaining base and camp security at Paya Lebar Air Base following a fake bomb threat. The transcript does not contain the specific measures, timelines, or operational constraints regarding security posture and personnel readiness that were requested. No further information on the Ministry's strategic position or management of incident aftermath was provided in this exchange.

results.title: Maintaining Detailed Product Traceability Information to Ensure Food Recall Notices Reach Small Retailers and Broader Public
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) directs implicated businesses to remove recalled products from all distribution points and verify compliance at premises, with non-compliance constituting an offence. To ensure recall notices reach small retailers and the public, SFA publishes press releases and monitors feedback for enforcement. Under the Food Safety and Security Act, importers and licensed food businesses at key distribution nodes must maintain traceability records by 2028. Businesses retain flexibility to adopt any system enabling swift, precise recalls, as SFA does not prescribe specific traceability systems.

results.title: Mandating Content Filters and Digital Watermarking for High-risk AI Tools
results.summary: IMDA will update policies on content filters and digital watermarking for high-risk AI tools as technical standards mature, rather than mandating them immediately. The Ministry emphasizes responsible AI deployment supported by the Model AI Governance Framework and testing tools like AI Verify. Singaporeans are protected by existing safeguards, including the Codes of Practice for Online Safety and the upcoming Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Act. Compliance audits for accessible AI models will follow the evolution of technical safeguards and regulatory frameworks.

results.title: Measures for Prevention, Detection and Early Treatment to Reverse Increase in Cancer Rates among People under 40
results.summary: The Government is implementing upstream population health measures to address rising cancer rates among those under 40, including discouraging smoking and alcohol, promoting healthy diets, and encouraging vaccinations. Early detection efforts focus on evidence-based screening programmes under Healthier SG. The Government will continue supporting research into cancer causes and management through the Singapore Translational Cancer Consortium to coordinate local researchers. Regular reviews and monitoring of prevention and care efforts will also be conducted.

results.title: Measuring Success in National R&D and Private Sector Collaborations and Safeguards to Prevent Loss of Singapore-based Expertise
results.summary: The Government measures collaboration success via Intellectual Property licensing, innovative product development, and new startup formation. National R&D institutions like Autonomous Universities and A*STAR build in-house capabilities while commercialising publicly funded inventions to strengthen the Research, Innovation and Enterprise ecosystem. Partnerships are deemed mutually beneficial as researchers commercialise IP with companies, spurring private sector R&D investment and creating high-value jobs. The Minister did not specify distinct metrics for differentiating a "project manager" versus "knowledge leader" roles or detail specific safeguards against expertise hollowing-out beyond the general emphasis on capacity building.

results.title: Mediation for Flexible Work Arrangement Requests
results.summary: Since the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests (TG-FWAR) took effect on 1 December 2024, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) has received one complaint. TAFEP has assisted the company in revising its processes to align with the guidelines. The Tripartite Alliance for Dispute Management does not handle TG-FWAR complaints; employees should approach TAFEP for assistance. Due to the single complaint received to-date, it is not possible to identify the top reasons for seeking mediation.

results.title: Outcomes of Lung Transplants Performed in Singapore and Maintaining Expertise in This Field
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2025, four lung transplant surgeries were performed in Singapore, with three recipients passing away and one recovering well. The Minister explained that low local prevalence of end-stage lung disease limits surgical volume compared to Western nations. To maintain expertise despite this low volume, the transplant team trains at high-volume overseas centres, participates in international workshops, and receives proctoring from Duke University Health System experts during procedures. Surgeons also retain technical skills by performing other thoracic surgeries and conducting animal model practice. Treating subsidised ASEAN patients was not explored as a solution to arrest skills erosion.

results.title: Preventing Unmeritorious Cases from Continuing to Receive Legal Aid
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, four legally aided persons were ordered to pay costs out of approximately 5,500 civil cases receiving aid. The Minister for Law confirmed that rigorous legal merits tests, including assessments of amenability to advice and reasonable prospects of success, are conducted before granting aid. Aid may be cancelled if new information affects case viability or if the applicant's conduct becomes unreasonable. These measures aim to prevent unmeritorious cases from continuing to receive support while ensuring access to justice for those with valid claims.

results.title: Quantity, Flat Type and Location of Current Stock of SBF Flats
results.summary: HDB currently holds close to 900 unselected Sale of Balance Flats (SBF) islandwide, comprising approximately 80 2-room Flexi flats and the remainder as 3-room or larger units. Following three SBF exercises conducted across 2024 and 2025, the Ministry stated that tracking quarter-on-quarter stock changes is not meaningful due to the stable take-up rate of around 90%. Unselected flats are subsequently re-offered in later sales exercises rather than accumulating as a growing stock.

results.title: Recommendation for One Dose of MMR Vaccine Before Travel for Infants Aged Six to 11 Months
results.summary: On 13 February 2026, the Communicable Diseases Agency issued guidance recommending that infants aged six to 11 months travelling to countries with measles outbreaks or high incidence receive an earlier dose of the Measles-Mumps-Rubella (MMR) vaccine. This dose must be administered at least two weeks before travel to reduce infection risk. Infants should still complete the standard two-dose MMR series according to the National Childhood Immunisation Schedule for long-term immunity. The guidance specifically addresses travel to affected regions rather than adopting a universal recommendation for all infants.

results.title: Recusal of Non-Muslim Board Members from Decisions Made on Singapore College of Islamic Studies' Islamic Curriculum
results.summary: The Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs clarified that the development of the Singapore College of Islamic Studies (SCIS) Islamic curriculum will be led by the Mufti, with support from religious leaders on the Board of Governors, Steering Committee, and Advisory Panel. The College will partner with Islamic institutions from Egypt, Jordan, and Morocco for curriculum design, while consulting interdisciplinary experts beyond Islamic Sciences to contextualize teachings for Singapore's diverse society. This approach ensures future religious teachers possess strong Islamic principles and an appreciation of modern societal context to serve the community effectively.

results.title: Redesigning Shelter Models to Better Meet Needs of Rough Sleepers
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) reports that approximately 75% of rough sleepers decline shelter entry primarily due to concerns over privacy, lack of personal space, storage, and specific rules. To address these barriers, MSF has redesigned shelter units with partitioned bed spaces, shelves, and wardrobes to enhance privacy and autonomy. Additionally, a $450,000 Partnership to Tackle Homelessness (PATH) fund was announced to support organisations in trialling innovative solutions for housing stability. MSF notes that while trust-building is essential for engagement, the primary driver for avoiding shelters remains the need for personal space rather than just safety or rule disagreements. Utilisation rates for the 730 transitional shelter beds were reported at 66% as of September last year.

results.title: Regulations for Advertisements that Make False Claims by Using AI-generated Images
results.summary: The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) enforces the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act against misleading advertisements, including those using AI-generated images, with no complaints received to date. CCS collaborates with e-commerce platforms to raise industry standards and has introduced the AI Markets Toolkit to help businesses ensure compliance. For property listings, the Council for Estate Agencies mandates that agents provide disclaimers when using AI to alter marketing materials and has taken enforcement action against non-compliant agents. The Council is currently reviewing its regulations to offer further specific guidance on AI usage in property advertisements. Consumers encountering false claims can seek assistance from the Consumers Association of Singapore.

results.title: Review of Fire Safety and Evacuation Risks Following Temporary Relaxation of Occupancy Cap for Private Residences
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that existing fire safety standards, including ventilated escape routes and fire compartments, remain sufficient for larger private residential properties (at least 90 square metres) under the temporary occupancy cap relaxation. Eligible owners must register with the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) and pay a $20 fee to increase their occupancy cap, enabling URA monitoring. Liveability and safety in common spaces are overseen by the Management Corporation Strata Title (MCST), while the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) conducts regular public education, safety days, and evacuation drills with Community Emergency and Engagement Committees. Hazard reporting triggers inspections, ensuring a holistic approach covering prevention, regulation, and community preparedness.

results.title: Review of International Baccalaureate Programme and Its Extension to More Mainstream Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education does not review the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP) curriculum as it is not part of the national syllabus, though student outcomes are monitored. Data indicates mainstream school IBDP students consistently outperformed global averages with high diploma award rates and positive 21st-century competency development. Consequently, there are no plans to extend the IBDP to additional mainstream schools while the GCE A-Level programme remains the relevant national curriculum. The Minister confirmed these positions during the parliamentary debate.

results.title: Reviewing Singapore's 2030 Solar Targets Following 2GWp Target Success and Planned Pathway towards Cleaner Energy Sources
results.summary: The Ministry increased the 2030 domestic solar deployment target to 3 GWp, announced in Budget 2026. To decarbonise the power system, the government is introducing next-generation gas-fired plants adaptable for low-carbon fuels like hydrogen, importing regional low-carbon electricity, and developing advanced nuclear capabilities. The Minister noted that Singapore remains alternative-energy disadvantaged, meaning decarbonisation pace depends on technological advancements and international cooperation. The specific review mechanism for the enhanced 2030 targets was not detailed in the response.

results.title: Singapore's Stance on Recognition of Somaliland as Independent and Sovereign State
results.summary: Singapore maintains its recognition of the Federal Republic of Somalia and has not altered its position regarding Somaliland's status as an independent and sovereign state. The Minister for Foreign Affairs confirmed that there has been no change to this policy. Consequently, Singapore does not currently recognize Somaliland as a separate nation.

results.title: Status of Step-up Measures to be Implemented by Carousell and Additional Consumer Protection Measures against E-commerce Scams
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs is assessing the effectiveness of Carousell's step-up measures, which were committed by 31 January 2026 under the E-Commerce Code of the Online Criminal Harms Act 2023, to prevent misuse of SingPass-verified accounts for e-commerce scams. The Ministry will provide an update on the implementation status when ready. It continues to monitor the scam situation on Carousell closely and will consider implementing further consumer protection measures if necessary. No specific additional measures have been announced at this time.

results.title: Stepped-up Vigilance or Measures for Zero-day Vulnerabilities Following UNC3886 Cyberattacks
results.summary: The Minister stated zero-day vulnerabilities cannot be fully eliminated as patches are unavailable, but the Government detects and prevents damage via the Cyber Security Agency and GovTech. Upon receiving credible exploit information, these agencies collaborate with vendors to confirm incidents and accelerate remediation. Critical system owners must conduct frequent security testing and threat hunting to enable timely detection. A layered defence approach combining perimeter controls with internal activity monitoring is required to robustly handle attacks before significant damage occurs.

results.title: Support Measures for Singapore-based Firms Navigating US Tech Restrictions and China Rare Earth Export Controls
results.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) and Singapore Customs issue advisories and conduct outreach to help firms navigate US tech restrictions and China's rare earth export controls. EnterpriseSG provides advisory tools through the Singapore Business Federation, while the Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce (SERT), established in April 2025, launched the Business Adaptation Grant in October 2025 to support supply chain diversification and compliance. The grant covers activities like reviewing legal risks and pursuing market diversification for eligible enterprises. The Government monitors global developments and will review support measures as needed.

results.title: Support for Social Workers to Deal With Children who Suffer from Vape and Etomidate-laced Vape Addiction
results.summary: Social workers supporting children in residential homes under Family Guidance Orders have access to specialised support services for vape and etomidate-laced vape addiction. The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed that these workers can utilise existing resources, including specific training in managing substance abuse and facilitating addiction recovery. No new programmes or timelines were announced during the exchange.

results.title: Supporting Lower-Income Students in Direct School Admission Applications to Ensure Equitable Talent Identification
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry of Education (MOE) provides Opportunity Fund grants to schools for subsidising co-curricular opportunities and runs the Junior Sports Academy for primary students with sporting potential, where selection relies on general motor ability without prior training. These initiatives aim to help lower-income students discover strengths that may lead to Direct School Admission (DSA) applications, though they are not designed to prepare them for DSA. For DSA, schools adopt a holistic selection approach considering potential and personal qualities beyond demonstrated achievements, recognising that talent manifests differently based on background and opportunities. The Minister clarified that these efforts do not target DSA preparation as it is not the main admission pathway.

results.title: Timeline for Public Bus Accessibility Feature Upgrades to Support Visually Impaired and Deaf Commuters
results.summary: As of 2026, approximately 20% of Singapore's public bus fleet features integrated Passenger Information Display Systems (PIDS) with visual and audio stop announcements, while another 20% has internal audio speakers. The Ministry does not plan to retrofit older buses, as they were not designed for such systems and retrofitting is deemed impractical; instead, all new buses will include PIDS. By 2030, around 70% of the fleet is projected to have at least one accessibility system as the fleet refreshes. For the remaining older buses, bus captains are trained to assist visually impaired and deaf commuters, and the Helping Hand scheme provides cards for passengers to request stop alerts.

results.title: Timeline of Indonesian Low-Carbon Electricity Projects and Impact on Future Household Electricity Prices
results.summary: Six Indonesian low-carbon electricity projects with Conditional Licences for 3GW are expected to begin commercial operations by the end of the decade. The Energy Market Authority notes that project developers are currently negotiating power purchase agreements with large business consumers, with no current mandate for households to purchase imported electricity. Retail plans for households will be offered on a willing-buyer-willing-seller basis at the developers' discretion, meaning there is no immediate impact on future household electricity prices.

results.title: Verifying Approval Process for Singapore's Exported Waste and Rejection Rates of Waste Export in 2024 and 2025
results.summary: Under the Hazardous Waste (Control of Export, Import and Transit) Act, the National Environment Agency (NEA) mandates that exporters obtain a Basel Permit secured via Prior Informed Consent from destination and transit states before exporting hazardous or controlled waste. This verification ensures compliance with the Basel Convention prior to shipment. For 2024 and 2025, NEA reports that zero waste exported under this permit and consent procedure was rejected or returned by destination countries. No further rejections or returns were recorded in the specified period.

results.title: Debate on Annual Budget Statement
results.summary: The Government supports Budget 2026 but acknowledges the need for structural changes to address worker anxiety, ageing demographics, and fiscal sustainability in a fragmented global order. While surpluses are attributed to capitalised infrastructure and tax revenue rather than over-collection, the Budget faces calls for redoubled efforts on AI readiness, including establishing a National AI Council chaired by the Prime Minister. Speakers urge structural, not incremental, adjustments to strengthen the intergenerational compact, normalizing career pauses, and ensuring flexible work rights. Specific concerns remain regarding coverage gaps for older children in family support schemes, the adequacy of retraining frameworks for displaced workers, and the need to prevent widening wage gaps between median and lower-wage groups.
